learning_outcomes.description: Course Specific Assessment - Professional Standard 1.1 Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of physical, social and intellectual development and characteristics of students and how these may affect learning
Course Specific Assessment - Professional Standard 1.2 Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of research into how students learn and the implications for teaching

Course Specific Assessment - Professional Standard 1.5 Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of strategies for differentiating teaching to meet the specific learning needs of students across the full range of abilities
Course Specific Assessment - Professional Standard 2.1 Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of the concepts, substance and structure of the content and teaching strategies of the teaching area
Course Specific Assessment - Professional Standard 2.2 Organise content into an effective learning and teaching sequence
Course Specific Assessment - Professional Standard 3.1 Set learning goals that provide achievable challenges for students of varying abilities and characteristics
Course Specific Assessment - Professional Standard 3.2 Plan lesson sequences using knowledge of student learning, content and effective teaching strategies
Course Specific Assessment - Professional Standard 3.3 Include a range of teaching strategies
Course Specific Assessment - Professional Standard 3.5 Demonstrate a range of verbal and nonverbal communication strategies to support student engagement
Course Specific Assessment - Professional Standard 3.6 Demonstrate broad knowledge of strategies that can be used to evaluate teaching programs to improve student learning
Course Specific Assessment - Professional Standard 3.7 Describe a broad range of strategies to engage parents/carers in the educative process
Course Specific Assessment - Professional Standard 4.1 Identify strategies to support inclusive student participation and engagement in classroom activities
Course Specific Assessment - Professional Standard 4.2 Demonstrate the capacity to organise classroom activities and provide clear directions

Course Specific Assessment - Professional Standard 5.1 Demonstrate understanding of assessment strategies, including informal and formal, diagnostic, formative and summative approaches to assess student learning
Course Specific Assessment - Professional Standard 5.2 Demonstrate an understanding of the purpose of providing timely and appropriate feedback to students about their learning.
Course Specific Assessment - Professional Standard 5.3 Demonstrate understanding of assessment moderation and its application to support consistent and comparable judgements of student learning.
Course Specific Assessment - Professional Standard 5.4 Demonstrate the capacity to interpret student assessment data to evaluate student learning and modify teaching practice

Course Specific Assessment - Professional Standard 5.5 Demonstrate understanding of strategies for reporting to students/carers and the purpose of keeping accurate and reliable records of student achievement
Course Specific Assessment - Professional Standard 6.3 Seek and apply constructive feedback from supervisors and teachers to improve teaching practices
Course Specific Assessment - Professional Standard 7.3 Understand strategies for working effectively, sensitively, and confidentially with parents/carers

Submissions must include:

  • An audio/video reflection of previous teaching experience and a subsequent video-based planning justification
  • Lesson plan submission
assessments.task_description:
To use evidence-informed reflections and relevant course content to inform planning that will best support student learning.

Use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) 

This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools.

Any use of AI or MT must: 

1. Be acknowledged appropriately in text and in reference list -

  • Consult UQ library website for how to do this 
  • https://guides.library.uq.edu.au/referencing/chatgpt-and-generative-ai-tools 

2. Be included in an appendix including a full transcript of any prompts and AI-generated responses.

assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
For pre-service teachers to demonstrate effective planning, teaching and assessment skills and evaluate impact of their teaching on student learning. 

Use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) 

The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
This course includes three teaching professional experiences: Introduction Secondary Professional Experience, Minor Professional Experience and UniPE Secondary. You must adopt the highest professional standards regarding personal presentation, interactions with school staff and students, attendance, punctuality and preparation. An inability to meet these requirements will result in an automatic failure for these professional experience despite achievement in other criteria. Full task details are available in Blackboard.

Use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) 

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

learning_activities.topic:
Front-ending assessment & School Based Teaching 1

  • What is inquiry-based pedagogies? 
  • Front-ending assessment – intro to GTPA and the role of EDUC3006 in supporting this process 
  • Course assessment overview  
  • How to design and use summative assessment tasks and rubrics to support the assessment and timely feedback of curriculum-based assessment criteria. PSTs and lecturer co-design summative assessment task and rubric aligned with curriculum achievement standards/relevant content descriptions.  
  • Model and demonstrate how the summative criteria is used to inform the diagnostic assessment for three designated students (these designated students represent the full range of abilities) to prepare for teaching next week  
  • Knowledge of how students’ varying physical, social, and intellectual development and individual characteristics may affect learning, planning and teaching 
  • Lesson planning for first teaching week in UniPE Secondary Professional Experience 
  • Organising content into an effective learning and teaching sequence 
  • Research (e.g., McDonald, 2019) is presented on the positive impact of building relationships on classroom behaviour and student outcomes. With their teaching partners, students discuss how these principles can be embedded effectively and meaningfully into their upcoming teaching session. Students then apply these principles into their planning for their teaching session.
  • Pitching introductory lesson at appropriate level - Designing learning experiences that reflect the summative requirements (i.e., modified version of final task as the initial learning experience). The intention of this planning process is to provide the teacher and students with evidence of where students are currently at in their learning (in relation to final task requirements) and inform next steps for the teacher’s planning and students actions to move closer towards personal and curriculum goals.
  • How to gather evidence to evaluate and inform teaching and learning. 
  • Providing strategies for reporting to students about their initial achievement 

Learning outcomes: L01, L04, L07, L10, L14, L15

learning_activities.topic:
School-Based Teaching 1

School-based teaching 1: PSTs enact lesson planning conducted in lecture time (see above) in an authentic school environment under the supervision of UQ course coordinator. 

  • Establishing rules and routines that support students’ learning opportunities.
  • Establishing positive relationships (specifically in self-assessment and small group activity time) with students using a variety of research informed (e.g., McDonald, 2019) verbal and non-verbal communication skills, and appropriate evaluation processes (Morgan, 2017, 2026) to support conducive learning behaviours and outcomes. Students also reflect on the effectiveness of their application of these principles in the reflective component proceeding the teaching session. 
  • Implement appropriate learning experiences for first lesson, that reflect the summative requirements (i.e., modified version of final task as the initial learning experience), as well as student-self-assessment, and small group-based and targeted activities. This enacted process provides teacher and students with evidence of where students are currently at in their learning (in relation to final task requirements). This information is then used to help inform the students of where they are going, and inform the teacher’s planning of areas to develop for the student to pitch an introductory lesson at the appropriate level and help them move closer towards desired personal and curriculum goals. 
  • Diagnostic assessment of three designated students’ (full range of abilities) physical performance. PSTs use co-designed criteria sheet (from Lecture activity) to perform a diagnostic assessment of their students. PSTs engage in a calibration exercise with the lecturer, to ensure they are assessing students at the appropriate level of mastery according to the achievement-standard informed criteria. Summative assessment judgements are then revisited and finalised in the Assessment and Moderation lecture. 
  • Using formative assessment to gather and interpret evidence to support timely and appropriate feedback and explicit teaching strategies aimed at enhancing students’ learning (for a full range of abilities). This specifically includes providing explicit feedback, revising tasks, and clarifying misconceptions based on students’ performances and responses during self-assessment and small group activities. 
  • Using rubrics to work with students to understand where they are currently at in their learning (diagnostic assessment) and to set ambitious learning goals 
  • Interpreting student formative assessment data and feedback from peers, teachers and students to inform next step planning and teaching 

Learning outcomes: L01, L04, L06, L08, L09, L13, L14, L15, L19

learning_activities.topic:
Applying Inquiry-Based Pedagogies

  • Calibrating assessment judgements (using summative assessment criteria) from diagnostic assessment in teaching week 1, to ensure appropriate benchmarking of current level of mastery. 
  • Applying research on Constraints-Led Approach (CLA) and implications for teaching and learning in a performance setting 
  • Using learning goals and success criteria to plan lessons using CLA 
  • Using knowledge of learning goals and designated students’ individual development and characteristics to organise content into an effective learning and teaching sequence. 
  • Interpreting student formative assessment data to evaluate student learning and inform future planning and teaching 
  • Modelling process of engaging school students in the self-evaluation and goal setting process, using curriculum-aligned rubrics in student-friendly language. This modelling helps prepare PSTs for enacting this process in UniPE Secondary professional experience with their students. 

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L04, L05, L06, L07, L10, L14

Interpreting student formative assessment data to evaluate student learning and inform future planning and teaching Modelling process of engaging school students in the self-evaluation and goal setting process, using curriculum-aligned rubrics in student-friendly language. This modelling helps prepare PSTs for enacting this process in UniPE Secondary professional experience with their students.

learning_activities.topic:
School-Based Teaching 2

PSTs enact lesson planning conducted in lecture time (see above) in an authentic school environment under the supervision of UQ course coordinator. 

  • Developing rules and routines that support students’ learning opportunities 
  • Reinforcing research-informed positive relationships with designated students through initial check-in discussions and revising of goals/self-assessment to support meaningful learning. 
  • Using rubrics to work with students to understand where they are currently at in their learning and to set/revise ambitious learning goals. 
  • Organising content and activities into an effective teaching and learning sequence 
  • Applying evidence-based practices (CLA) to develop teaching and learning outcomes 
  • Using formative assessment to gather and interpret evidence to support timely and appropriate feedback and explicit teaching strategies aimed at enhancing students’ learning (for a full range of student abilities). This includes providing explicit feedback, revising tasks, and clarifying misconceptions based on students’ performances and responses during self-assessment and small group activities. 
  • Interpreting student formative assessment data and feedback from peers, teachers and students to inform next step planning and teaching. 

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L04, L05, L06, L08, L09, L13, L14, L15, L17, L19

learning_activities.topic:
Feedback, Differentiation and Motivation

  • Embedding appropriate feedback and formative assessment practices within a mastery-oriented approach to learning. Explaining and modelling how the TARGET framework (Morgan, 2017, 2026) can support a research-informed pedagogical approach to support positive student teacher relationships through sensitive, structured and learning-supportive practices (e.g., self-determined differentiated tasks, heterogenous and small grouping, self-referenced evaluation, etc). With their teaching partners, students discuss how these TARGET principles can be embedded effectively and meaningfully into their upcoming teaching session. Students then apply these principles into their planning for their teaching session. 
  • Using research on different types of feedback and the impact of these on student learning 
  • Evaluating own feedback practices and the impact of these practices on student learning  
  • How to provide timely and appropriate feedback to enhance students’ learning 
  • How to provide differentiated feedback that is relevant to students’ needs and current competency level 
  • Using formative assessment data to inform next-step teaching 
  • Using a variety of teaching strategies to create challenging and inclusive learning opportunities 
  • Using formative assessment data to plan and design differentiated learning experiences to meet specific learning needs of students with a range of abilities 

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06, L07, L08, L10, L12

learning_activities.topic:
Assessment, Moderation and Reporting

  • Revisiting summative assessment task and rubric design to support curriculum-aligned judgements of students’ performances. Summative assessments judgements from UniPE Secondary Professional Experience (1) are revisited and finalised.  
  • Making judgements of students’ performances (summative assessment)  
  • How to provide timely and appropriate feedback to enhance students’ learning 
  • How to provide differentiated feedback that is relevant to students’ needs and current competency level 
  • Engaging in moderation processes to support consistent and comparable judgements of student learning 
  • How to effectively communicate to students their level of achievement 
  • Using formative assessment data to plan and design differentiated learning experiences to meet specific learning needs of students with a range of abilities 

Learning outcomes: L01, L03, L04, L05, L07, L09, L11, L14, L15, L16, L18

learning_activities.topic:
Evaluating Impact of Teaching on Student Learning

  • Reporting on student achievement outcomes to engage parents/carers in the educative process 
  • Using evidence of three designated students’ learning outcomes to evaluate effectiveness of differentiated teaching practices 
  • Using evidence from peers, supervisors, and personal reflections to determine effectiveness of teaching on student learning 
  • Using evidence from teaching experience to inform next steps in development of future teaching  

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L12, L14, L17, L20

learning_activities.topic:
Assessment & Professional Experience Preparation

Finalise assessment of student performances. Using evidence to support evaluation of teaching and learning. Preparation and support for final assessment task.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06, L07, L08, L09, L10, L11, L12, L13, L14, L15, L16, L17, L18, L19, L20
learning_activities.topic_description: Assessment & Professional Experience Preparation Finalise assessment of student performances. Using evidence to support evaluation of teaching and learning. Preparation and support for final assessment task.

learning_activities.topic:
Professional Experience Preparation

·     Revise rules, routines and behaviour management strategies to support learning

·     Students investigate behaviour management policy and processes at their respective practicum schools

·     Revise and discuss broader strategies for developing effective relationships to support the learning process

·     Discuss strategies and resources for developing engaging classroom-based learning experiences

·     Discuss strategies for engaging sensitively and effectively with parents and carers to support students

·     Outline professionalism requirements for professional experience (including attire, attendance, arrival and departure times, duties, professional communication with staff and students, showing initiative, etc).

·     Discuss strategies for working effectively with supervisor (clear communication and understanding supervisor’s specific expectations, applying supervisor feedback to improve subsequent teaching and learning experiences).

·     How to maximise learning opportunities during the professional experience (including observing other teachers, engaging in assessment practices, engaging in co-curricular and school activities)

  • Outline of support processes available during placement.

Learning outcomes: L11, L12, L13, L19, L20
learning_activities.topic_description: Professional Experience Preparation ·     Revise rules, routines and behaviour management strategies to support learning ·     Students investigate behaviour management policy and processes at their respective practicum schools ·     Revise and discuss broader strategies for developing effective relationships to support the learning process ·     Discuss strategies and resources for developing engaging classroom-based learning experiences ·     Discuss strategies for engaging sensitively and effectively with parents and carers to support students ·     Outline professionalism requirements for professional experience (including attire, attendance, arrival and departure times, duties, professional communication with staff and students, showing initiative, etc). ·     Discuss strategies for working effectively with supervisor (clear communication and understanding supervisor’s specific expectations, applying supervisor feedback to improve subsequent teaching and learning experiences). ·     How to maximise learning opportunities during the professional experience (including observing other teachers, engaging in assessment practices, engaging in co-curricular and school activities) Outline of support processes available during placement.
